examples.title: Clock Divider by N
examples.description: Verilog module to divide input clock by any integer N
examples.explanation: This module divides the input clock by an even integer. It uses a counter that toggles the output when reaching half the division factor, creating the divided clock signal.

examples.title: D Flip-Flop with Synchronous Reset
examples.description: VHDL code for a D flip-flop with setup/hold and synchronous reset
examples.explanation: This D flip-flop captures input D on rising clock edge. Reset is synchronous - it only works on clock edge, ensuring proper timing behavior and avoiding metastability issues.

examples.title: Two-Phase Clock Generator
examples.description: Verilog module generating non-overlapping two-phase clocks
examples.explanation: Generates two non-overlapping clock phases used in two-phase clocking systems. The gap between phases prevents race conditions in sequential circuits.

examples.title: Metastability Mitigation - Synchronizer
examples.description: Algorithm for 2-stage synchronizer to reduce metastability
examples.explanation: The first flip-flop may become metastable but has one clock period to settle. The second flip-flop captures a stable value, dramatically improving MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures).

examples.title: Clock Gating Cell
examples.description: Verilog example of integrated clock gating to reduce power
examples.explanation: Clock gating stops clock switching in idle blocks, eliminating dynamic power consumption. Integrated clock gating cells (ICG) are preferred over simple AND gates to avoid clock glitches.
